title: Mammea suriga
intro: Mammea suriga, also known as Suriga or Philippine Cupped Mammea, is a plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Clusiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Mammea suriga include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, such as mammea and surigagenin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Mammea suriga is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
uses: Mammea suriga is reported to have various medicinal uses due to its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Mammea suriga is also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling. Its leaves and bark are sometimes used to create teas or infusions for medicinal purposes. Further research is needed to fully understand and confirm the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
